column2: Meaning: a book that is so interesting or exciting that you want to keep on reading
column3: Examples: The new Thursday Murder Club novel is a real page-turner!

column1: don't judge a book by its cover
column2: Meaning: don't form an opinion about something (or someone) just based on appearance
column3: Examples: He looks shy, but he's actually a lot of fun. Don't judge a book by its cover

column2: Meaning: someone who's hard to get to know
column3: Examples: I've worked with Chris for years, but he's still a closed book to me

column1: to be on the same page
column2: Meaning: to have the same understanding or the same opinion as someone else
column3: Examples: We're both on the same page when it comes to cats over dogs as pets.

column1: to turn over a new leaf
column2: Meaning: to change behaviour and start behaving better
column3: Examples: He was so lazy last year, but this semester he's turned over a new leaf and is studying hard.

column2: Meaning: to understand the hidden meaning in what someone is saying or writing
column3: Examples: She said she was fine, but reading between the lines, I could tell she was upset.
